source_pr.title: importinto: revert "use one writer for each kv group for all concurrent encoder"
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

This reverts pr #47185, using 1 writer for a kv group for all encode routine, will mix kvs(such as data) in different chunk into one sorted kv file, when write to tikv, external engine might switch from 1 file to another frequently as the ordered kvs might store in different files, and only a small part of it is continuous in one file, it causes import speed extremely slow

![image](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/assets/3312245/5bb3f35f-38ef-4841-a113-0a398d6a85c3)

Issue Number: ref #46704, #47572

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
this pr only revert another pr, so existing test should be enough, we only do a simple manual test here:
- import with global sort, should work. for a case of 150g, takes 70m after vs 461m before
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->  

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Revert of PR #47185 due to severe global-sort import performance regression (not a clear flaky signature).
failure_signature: global-sort import performance regression after writer/concurrency change (revert)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR is a revert motivated by a large performance regression in global sort import (mixing KV groups into the same sorted file caused external engine to switch files frequently and become extremely slow). While the catalog captured a `race = "on"` / sorting-related snippet, the patch itself is not a targeted stabilization of a specific flaky test behavior. To avoid misclassification, we keep the conservative fallback category.
fix_pattern: Revert the "one writer per KV group for all concurrent encoders" change to avoid mixing disjoint KV ranges into the same sorted file (which caused external engine file-switch thrashing and massive import slowdowns).
analysis:
PR title: importinto: revert "use one writer for each kv group for all concurrent encoder"
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/47666
Triage sources: manual_triage, explicit_flaky_signal, determinism_stabilization
Triage summary: race = "on", | slices.Sort(allData) (tests/realtikvtest/importintotest4/global_sort_test.go)
Changed files (7): br/pkg/lightning/backend/external/writer.go, pkg/disttask/importinto/encode_and_sort_operator.go, pkg/disttask/importinto/encode_and_sort_operator_test.go, pkg/disttask/importinto/scheduler.go, pkg/executor/importer/BUILD.bazel, pkg/executor/importer/chunk_process.go, pkg/executor/importer/chunk_process_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
